businessSummary.oneLiner: Government and commercial AI data analytics leader built on three pillars: Gotham (defense), Foundry (enterprise), and AIP (AI platform)
businessSummary.description: Palantir builds AI-powered data analytics platforms for governments and enterprises. FY2025 revenue hit $4.48B (+56%), with U.S. commercial revenue crossing $500M as the company proves it can scale beyond defense. The company recently expanded its defense AI reach with the Keel partnership on the Navy's ShipOS submarine production initiative. The stock is up 1,860% over three years but has experienced 10+ drawdowns of 20% or more during that period.

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: More than 10x the S&P 500 average of 22x. Motley Fool calls it a 2,500% premium to the index. Extreme even by software standards (sector avg ~35x)

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 EPS estimate of $1.34. Growth premium is steep but reflects accelerating revenue trajectory

keyMetrics.name: Price-to-Sales
keyMetrics.interpretation: Investors pay ~$80 for every $1 of Palantir revenue. Typical SaaS companies trade at 10-15x sales

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (YoY)
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 $4.48B. FY2026 guidance $7.18-7.20B (+60%). U.S. commercial revenue projected +115%

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Top-tier among SaaS companies. Achieving high profitability alongside rapid growth

keyMetrics.name: FCF Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: FCF of $2.1B. Best-in-class cash generation for software. However, FCF yield is under 1% at current valuation

growthDrivers.title: PLTR rose while market dropped on Fed hawkishness
growthDrivers.description: While the S&P 500 fell 1.4% after the Fed cut rate projections, PLTR climbed 1.9%. Defense AI demonstrated independent momentum against macro headwinds.

growthDrivers.title: UBS raises target to $200, Wedbush holds $230
growthDrivers.description: UBS analyst Karl Keirstead raised his price target from $180 to $200 (+29%). Wedbush maintained its $230 Buy target. Wall Street bullishness is accelerating.

growthDrivers.title: NYT: 'Silicon Valley bet on war. It's being rewarded.'
growthDrivers.description: The New York Times spotlighted Palantir as a key success story in defense AI investing. Defense tech is going mainstream.

growthDrivers.title: Navy ShipOS $448M: AI applied to submarine production
growthDrivers.description: Keel joined Palantir on the Navy's ShipOS initiative, extending AI applications to submarine production lines and expanding defense AI scope.

growthDrivers.title: $11.2B revenue backlog + $10B Army contract
growthDrivers.description: The $10B Army contract is reshaping defense software. $11.2B backlog provides multi-year revenue visibility.

growthDrivers.title: FY2026 revenue guidance $7.18-7.20B (+60%), growth accelerating
growthDrivers.description: From FY2025 $4.48B to FY2026 guidance of $7.18-7.20B. Growth rate is accelerating, not decelerating. U.S. commercial revenue projected to jump 115%.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 20+ years of government and defense AI track record. Security clearances create near-impenetrable barriers to entry. ShipOS extends AI from the battlefield to military production.

moats.description: Holds highest-level security clearances with the DoD and intelligence agencies. Competitors cannot easily replicate this.
AI deployed in submarine production and naval shipyards. Expanding beyond the battlefield into military manufacturing.
Sovereign AI OS combined with NVIDIA hardware ecosystem. Positioning to become the standard for government AI infrastructure globally.

competitors.name: Microsoft (Azure AI)
competitors.detail: Cloud AI platform taking a generalist approach vs Palantir's specialized analytics

competitors.name: Snowflake
competitors.detail: Data lakehouse expanding into analytics platforms

competitors.name: C3.ai
competitors.detail: Enterprise AI competitor trailing Palantir in scale and execution

recentNews.headline: Palantir partners with Moder on AI mortgage platform
recentNews.significance: Freedom Mortgage is the first pilot customer for AI-powered mortgage operations. Proves commercial expansion beyond defense into financial services.

recentNews.headline: Mizuho reiterates Outperform after exec meeting
recentNews.significance: Mizuho reaffirmed Outperform rating with $195 target after management access. Analyst consensus rises to $196.32.

recentNews.headline: Pentagon pushes 6-month wind-down of Anthropic Claude from Maven
recentNews.significance: DoD wants to remove Anthropic's Claude from Maven within 6 months, but military users say it's not that simple. Palantir Maven contracts exceed $1B.

recentNews.headline: UBS raises price target to $200 from $180, maintains Buy
recentNews.significance: Karl Keirstead sees 29% upside from current levels. Combined with Wedbush at $230 (+48%), Wall Street's bull case strengthens.

recentNews.headline: Keel joins Palantir on Navy ShipOS initiative ($448M)
recentNews.significance: AI now extends to submarine production. Maximizing defense AI application scope.

risks.title: Extreme valuation: P/E 228x, P/S 80x
risks.description: Trading at 10x the S&P 500 average P/E. Motley Fool describes it as a 2,500% premium to the index. FCF yield under 1%, meaning it would take over 100 years to recoup an investment at current cash generation.

risks.title: Persistent insider selling
risks.description: CEO Alex Karp sold 493K shares ($66M) on 2/20. Director Stephen Cohen sold 327K shares ($43.7M), disposing of 99.8% of his holdings. Management is actively taking profits at current levels.

risks.title: Pentagon Anthropic Claude removal risk
risks.description: The DoD is pushing to remove Anthropic's Claude from Maven within 6 months. Palantir's Maven contracts exceed $1B. Switching AI engines could be costly and time-consuming.

risks.title: Government revenue concentration + UK NHS privacy backlash
risks.description: Privacy pushback in the UK threatens NHS contract. High government revenue dependency makes the company vulnerable to political risk.

risks.title: Stock dilution
risks.description: Stock-based compensation dilutes shares by roughly 5% annually. Per-share value erosion eats into long-term returns.

risks.title: Intensifying AI competition
risks.description: OpenAI announced plans to sell AI to U.S. defense agencies via AWS. Microsoft, AWS, and Google are all investing heavily in AI platforms.

buyReasons.title: Moder mortgage partnership: AI expansion from defense to finance
buyReasons.rationale: Freedom Mortgage pilot proves Palantir's commercial reach beyond defense. U.S. commercial revenue projected to grow 115%.

buyReasons.title: Bucked the market sell-off: defense AI shows independent momentum
buyReasons.rationale: Rose 1.9% while S&P 500 dropped 1.4%. Defense AI premium is insulated from macro headwinds.

buyReasons.title: Three firms simultaneously bullish: UBS $200, Wedbush $230, Mizuho $195
buyReasons.rationale: Consensus target $196.32 (+26.1%). 16 Buy and 2 Strong Buy ratings out of 28 analysts.

buyReasons.title: $11.2B backlog + 60% growth acceleration
buyReasons.rationale: Best-in-class revenue visibility. FY2026 guidance of $7.18-7.20B shows growth is accelerating.

analystOpinions.notableComment: UBS raised to $200 (+29%), Wedbush holds $230 (+48%), Mizuho reiterates Outperform at $195. 16 Buy + 2 Strong Buy out of 28 analysts. Moder mortgage partnership accelerates commercial momentum.

overallOpinion: Palantir partnered with Moder to build an AI mortgage platform, with Freedom Mortgage as its first client. The company is proving it can expand beyond defense, and U.S. commercial revenue is projected to grow 115% this year.

The Pentagon is trying to remove Anthropic's Claude from Maven, but military users are pushing back. With Palantir's Maven contracts exceeding $1B, the AI engine transition adds near-term uncertainty.

UBS ($200), Wedbush ($230), and Mizuho ($195) are all bullish simultaneously. At 228x P/E, it trades at a 2,500% premium to the S&P 500, but 60%+ growth acceleration justifies a Buy on pullbacks. Currently just below the 50-day SMA at $158.

investmentVerdict.summary: The Moder mortgage partnership proves Palantir can extend its AI platform beyond defense into financial services. Three major firms are simultaneously bullish with a consensus target of $196 (+26%). While the 228x P/E is extreme, FY2026 guidance of $7.18-7.20B (+60%) and U.S. commercial revenue growth of 115% support the premium. Monitor the Pentagon's Anthropic wind-down for near-term risk, but the overall case favors buying on dips.

threads.hook: Palantir: from submarines to mortgages, defense AI goes commercial
threads.text:
$PLTR $155.68(+1.9%)

Palantir partnered with Moder to build an AI mortgage platform. Freedom Mortgage is the pilot client. The company is proving it can scale from submarines to home loans, and US commercial revenue is on track for +115%.

Pentagon wants Anthropic's Claude out of Maven but military users are pushing back hard. Maven contracts exceed $1B. Mizuho reiterated Outperform at $195 after management meetings, calling Palantir "unlike anything else in software."

UBS $200, Wedbush $230. P/E 228x at 2,500% premium to S&P, but 60%+ growth acceleration backs it. Rose 1.9% while S&P fell 1.4%. Buy. Target $196(+26%).

empathy.hook: PLTR holders: market crashed, you went up. You earned this.
empathy.text:
$PLTR $155.68(+1.9%)

S&P dropped 1.4% and PLTR rose. The Moder mortgage AI partnership was announced. For those anxious about defense-only dependence, this is the diversification you've been waiting for.

Concerns remain. Pentagon Anthropic removal from Maven, $1B+ contracts in flux. But military pushing back on the switch proves how essential Palantir has become.

228x P/E. Karp selling. But 3 major firms bullish (UBS $200, Wedbush $230, Mizuho $195). This stock has dropped 20%+ ten times in 3 years. Recovered every single time.

$155.68, target $196(+26%). Buy. The ones who held through turbulence came out ahead.
